title: AGF Grid components
description: S81 KABOOM-GRID-POSITION. Reusable grid primitives — applicable to bomberman-style arcade games, roguelikes, tower-defence, RTS. Project gameplay code consumes `runtime.grid.occupants` (provided by GridOccupancySystem) instead of running raw world queries each frame.

gridComponent.description: Scene-level grid configuration. Conventionally placed on a singleton 'grid-config' entity; `engine check` rejects scenes with more than one Grid component. `cellSize` is in world units (typically 1). `sizeX`/`sizeZ` are inclusive cell counts along the X and Z axes; `originX`/`originZ` give the world position of the (0, 0) cell centre.

gridPositionComponent.description: Cell coordinates of an entity on the scene's Grid. `engine check` rejects out-of-bounds values (`AGF_GRID_POSITION_OUT_OF_BOUNDS`). Renderer-side Transform.position is derived from this by GridMovementSystem; gameplay code never writes Transform.position directly for grid entities.

gridMoverComponent.description: S81 KABOOM-GRID-MOVER. Smoothly tweens an entity between grid cells. Project gameplay code writes `queuedDirection` from input / AI; GridMovementSystem owns `currentLerp`, `targetGx`, `targetGz` and writes Transform.position. `speed` is cells per second.

gridOccupantComponent.description: Declares how an entity participates in grid queries. `layer` lets multiple entities share a cell on different layers (floor / wall / blast). `blocksMovement` and `blocksBlast` are sample-game-agnostic predicates GridOccupancySystem exposes via runtime.grid.occupants(...) and runtime.grid.blocked(...).
